title: New York Fed Report Shows Rises in Auto Loan Originations and Balances

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The New York Fed released its Q2 2014 Household Debt and Credit Report showing auto loan originations at the highest level in eight years, with auto loan balances increasing for the 13th consecutive quarter. Total household indebtedness was essentially flat at $11.63 trillion, down 0.2% from the prior quarter and 8.2% below the Q3 2008 peak of $12.68 trillion.
key_points: Auto loan originations reached highest level since 2006
Auto loan balances increased for 13th consecutive quarter
Total household debt: $11.63 trillion (down $18 billion from Q1 2014)
Mortgage debt declined $69 billion to $8.09 trillion
90+ day delinquency rate dropped to 4.5% from 4.8% in Q1 2014
New foreclosures at lowest level in the data series
why_it_matters: This quarterly household debt report from the NY Fed provides baseline data on consumer credit conditions. The strength in auto lending and declining delinquency rates indicate improving household credit health.
macro_context: This is a historical quarterly data release from Q2 2014 (August 2014 publication). The data predates the pandemic era and subsequent monetary policy cycles. As historical reference data on household debt trends, it has limited relevance to current macro analysis as of 2026.
market_structure_context: Not applicable — this report covers household debt and credit data (auto loans, mortgages, delinquency rates) rather than market structure, trading venue operations, market plumbing, or settlement dynamics.
